name: Salazar Valley Loop
name_original: Ruta Río Anduña – Ochagavía, Ezcároz, Oronz, Esparza de Salazar, Sarriés – Ruta Circular
description: This difficult 18.8-mile touring cycling route explores the Navarrese Pyrenees, connecting charming villages and offering stunning mountain
region_intro: Hiking trails around Ochagavía/Otsagabia are set within the Navarrese Pyrenees, offering diverse terrain for outdoor activities. The region is characterized by green landscapes, mountains, and dense forests, including the Irati Forest, known as Europe's second best-preserved beech-fir forest. Rivers like the Zatoya and Anduña converge in the area, and the picturesque Irabia Reservoir is a notable feature. This environment provides a range of hiking opportunities across varied elevations. Best hiking trails around Ochagavía/Otsagabia The most popular hiking route is Sierra de Irati – circular to the Irabia Reservoir, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the dense beech forests surrounding the Irabia Reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pic d'Orhy Trail from Port de Larrau, a difficult 2.7 miles (4.4 km) path. This trail ascends to Pic d'Orhy, the highest point in the Basque Country, offering panoramic views of the Pyrenees. Local hikers also love the Old Irati Houses Trail, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail leading through the Irati Forest, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Ochagavía/Otsagabia is defined by the extensive Irati Forest, Pyrenean mountain landscapes, and river valleys. The network of routes offers options for easy walks, moderate excursions, and challenging ascents. The routes in Ochagavía/Otsagabia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Ochagavía/Otsagabia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Ochagavía/Otsagabia?
A: There are over 45 hiking trails around Ochagavía/Otsagabia, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels. These include 11 easy routes, 24 moderate trails, and 14 more challenging hikes.

Q: What natural landmarks or points of interest can I explore while hiking?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/963896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Holzarte Footbridge&lt;/a&gt;, which offers spectacular views over the Olhadubi Gorge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2706541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic d'Orhy&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point in the Basque Country, provides incredible panoramic views. Additionally, the picturesque Irabia Reservoir is a central feature of many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964962171/sierra-de-irati-circular-to-the-irabia-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Irati – circular to the Irabia Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; trail.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking trails in Ochagavía/Otsagabia?
A: Yes, there are several easy paths suitable for families. The Irati Forest offers shorter, less strenuous options like the Zabaleta Forest Trail and Errekaidorra's interpretative path. A good example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968851785/paraisos-erlan-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paraísos - Erlan Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 5 km route through the forest.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in the region?
A: Ochagavía/Otsagabia and the surrounding Irati Forest are generally welcoming to dogs, making it a great destination for outdoor enthusiasts with pets. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to check for any specific local regulations or signage on individual trails.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many trails in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964962171/sierra-de-irati-circular-to-the-irabia-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Irati – circular to the Irabia Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the dense beech forests around the reservoir. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968307200/cascada-el-cubo-circular-by-the-zepa-roncesvalles-sierra-irati" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada El Cubo – circular by the ZEPA Roncesvalles-Sierra Irati&lt;/a&gt;, which includes a waterfall.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, you can find trails that lead to beautiful waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968307200/cascada-el-cubo-circular-by-the-zepa-roncesvalles-sierra-irati" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cascada El Cubo – circular by the ZEPA Roncesvalles-Sierra Irati&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.7 km route that takes you to the Cascada El Cubo, a notable natural attraction in the area.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Ochagavía/Otsagabia?
A: The Irati Forest and surrounding Pyrenees are beautiful year-round. Spring offers vibrant blooms, summer provides pleasant temperatures for hiking, and autumn is spectacular with the changing colors of the beech trees. Even winter offers opportunities for snow sports and serene snowy landscapes, though some higher trails may be less accessible.

Q: What makes the Irati Forest a special place for hiking?
A: The Irati Forest is renowned as the second best-preserved beech-fir forest in Europe. Its magical and protected environment offers lush vegetation, diverse wildlife, and a vast network of trails. It transforms beautifully with the seasons, providing a unique experience whether you visit for vibrant autumn colors, spring blooms, or summer tranquility.

Q: Are there any cultural or historical sites to visit near the trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, the village of Ochagavía itself is a cultural gem with its cobblestone streets, traditional stone houses, and a medieval bridge. You can also visit the 12th-century Romanesque Sanctuary of Our Lady of Muskilda, located on Mount Muskilda, which offers both historical significance and scenic views.

Q: Which trails offer the best panoramic views of the Pyrenees?
A: For breathtaking panoramic views, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e968755480/pic-d-orhy-trail-from-port-de-larrau" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic d'Orhy Trail from Port de Larrau&lt;/a&gt; is highly recommended. This challenging route ascends to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2706541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic d'Orhy&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point in the Basque Country, providing expansive vistas of the Pyrenees, the Bay of Biscay, and surrounding regions.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Ochagavía/Otsagabia?
A: The trails in Ochagavía/Otsagabia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning natural landscapes, the well-maintained paths through the Irati Forest, and the diverse range of routes suitable for all abilities.

Q: Is it possible to hike in Ochagavía/Otsagabia during winter?
A: Yes, winter hiking is possible, and the region transforms into a beautiful snowy landscape. While some higher mountain trails might require specialized gear or be inaccessible, many lower-elevation forest paths remain enjoyable. The area is also suitable for cross-country skiing during the colder months.
tips: A pretty and typical village in this part of Navarre. It was destroyed in a fire during the French invasion in 1794, but was rebuilt in the mid-19th century. Its architecture is notable, as are the typical sloping roofs of the houses. Don't miss the beautiful medieval bridge at the entrance to the village. Also, check out the San Juan Evangelista church and the shrine of Our Lady of Muskilda.

One of the fourteen towns that make up the Salazar Valley. It has cobbled streets and Pyrenean houses with steeply sloping roofs and flat tiles. It is a cattle town, with sheep and cows, and potatoes are also grown. A large part of its extension is forest, with pine, beech and oak.
A small charming town next to the Salazar River. Close to the Irati Forest, a must see. Here you can try the typical snacks of the valley, such as migas de pastor, sheep meat, beef and wild boar.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is classified as a "difficult bike ride," demanding very good physical fitness from cyclists. It features a significant elevation gain of approximately 760 meters over its 30.2-kilometer length.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Based on the route data, it typically takes around 10,091 seconds, which is approximately 2 hours and 48 minutes, to complete this touring bicycle route. This duration can vary depending on your fitness level and stops along the way.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features paved surfaces, making it suitable for touring bikes despite the challenging elevation. While some segments of other routes in the region may be unpaved, this specific circular route is predominantly on asphalt.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or scenic highlights along the route?
faqs.answer: The route winds through the stunning landscapes of the Navarrese Pyrenees. You'll pass through charming settlements like Otsagabia (Ochagavía) and Ezcároz, and can see the Stone Bridge in Oronz. The area is rich in natural beauty, being located next to the Irati Forest, and offers impressive vistas where forests appear to merge with the horizon.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The trail research does not specify the best time of year. However, given its location in the Spanish Pyrenees, spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery for cycling, while summer can be warm and winter may bring snow or colder conditions at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Ochagavía, which serves as an excellent base for various routes. While specific parking locations are not detailed, Ochagavía is a village with services, suggesting parking options would be available within or near the settlement.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: There is no information in the provided sources indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this specific route. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify whether this touring cycling route is dog-friendly. For cycling routes, dogs are generally not recommended to accompany cyclists unless they are in a dedicated trailer or carrier, due to safety concerns on paved roads and with traffic.

faqs.question: Are there places to refuel or find refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: The starting and ending point, Ochagavía, offers services such as restaurants and rural accommodations. While the route itself is challenging and covers several villages, it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, as specific cafes or refreshment stops directly on the route are not detailed.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant sections of the Senda Pirenaica - E32 (28%) and the Otsagabia bidea (17%), among others like Sarria bidea (11%) and Landetako bidea (4%).

faqs.question: What should I pack for this challenging touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and elevation gain, you should pack essentials for a challenging touring bike ride. This includes sufficient water and snacks, appropriate cycling gear, a repair kit for your bike, sun protection, and layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather. A first-aid kit is also recommended.
